text.title: pytest-django Antipatterns
text.summary: Common test mistakes and LLM-specific failure modes in Django/pytest projects.

antipattern.description: Testing internal method calls instead of observable behavior.
antipattern.reason: Tests that assert on _validate_items was called break on any refactoring that renames the method, even if behavior is unchanged. Test the outcome (exception raised, object created) not the path.

antipattern.description: Shared mutable state on test class attributes (class-level list or dict).
antipattern.reason: Tests execute in arbitrary order; shared state causes order-dependent failures. Use pytest fixtures — each test gets a fresh instance.

antipattern.description: Re-creating fixtures inline instead of reusing shared conftest fixtures.
antipattern.reason: Inline creation duplicates logic and diverges from the shared fixture over time. LLMs must read conftest.py first and reuse user, api_client, auth_client.
Masks migration drift — agents update models without re-running migrations, and existing DB state hides the missing column. CI must always --create-db.

antipattern.description: Calling mocker.patch with the module the agent read, not the module where the SUT imports the symbol.
antipattern.reason: Patching the wrong import path produces a test that always passes regardless of the mock — silent zero coverage.

rule.statement: Require agents to read tests/conftest.py before generating any test. Prompt: "Read tests/conftest.py first. Reuse existing fixtures."
rule.rationale: LLMs default to inline creation rather than reuse; reading the file first surfaces the available fixture names.

rule.statement: Require --strict-markers and a lint rule that finds DB-touching code in tests without @pytest.mark.django_db.
rule.rationale: LLMs omit the marker; the resulting error is clear but wastes tokens iterating. Catch it in lint, not at runtime.
